description: F&B establishments promoting Ramadan iftar dining experiences with pricing, location, and reservation details in bilingual Arabic-English captions.
structural_recipe: Lead with 'رمضان مبارك' (Ramadan greeting). State restaurant name and cuisine type. Include location with pin emoji. Display per-person price in SAR. Add clear reservation contact (phone/WhatsApp). Use bilingual formatting (Arabic primary, English secondary). Emphasize iftar (breaking fast) positioning. Keep captions concise with high visual hierarchy.
why_it_works: Ramadan is peak dining season in Saudi Arabia with heightened spending on premium experiences. Bilingual captions reach both Arabic-native and expatriate audiences. Explicit pricing removes friction. Direct reservation CTAs capitalize on time-sensitive holiday demand. Location tagging leverages local search behavior during Ramadan celebration period.
transplantation_caveats: Only relevant during Ramadan season (9th Islamic month). Non-halal restaurants risk negative engagement in Saudi market. Premium pricing may alienate budget-conscious segments. Iftar-specific positioning loses relevance outside fasting hours. Regional variations in iftar traditions across Saudi regions require localized messaging.
cultural_constraints: Respect Islamic values in Ramadan messaging—avoid secular framing
Ensure halal certification visibility for F&B offerings
Honor prayer times; adjust reservation messaging around Maghrib
Acknowledge family-centric dining culture during Ramadan
Use appropriate religious etiquette in Arabic language choices
